# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3' # specify the version of docker compose yml file (should be 3 for most recent versions like Docker Compose v3)
services:   # define new service(s). The services in here can then by run inside a specific order or with additional arguments as specified. If multiple are defined, only one is started at any time but the others might still start up depending on your config/startup command for other applications – just make sure they're running within docker network
    elk_stack: # name of service in compose file must be unique to avoid conflict and should match with image names inside Dockerfile or .yml files. It can include alphanumeric chars as well symbols if needed but are not recommended unless necessary for the case you're specifying
      image: elasticsearch:latest # replace this by your desired ELK stack version, it could be something like 'docker-registry/elk_stack', or even a full tag name ‘elasticsearch:7.12’ etc... and if using private registry then provide correct details of the form - username / password pair
      network_mode: "<your custom service's internal port>" # Specify your own setup, default value should be 'serviceName'. Use <name-of-myapp/myservice>  or  mynetworks name. This is just to ensure communication among services within same docker networks only when you provide a network mode
      restart_policy: {}    # specify how service restarts on failures and set up auto retry in case of failure (optional) but not recommended due security implications unless required for specific use-case – should be commented out by default. Also, depending upon application nature different policies can still apply if needed - like always or unless_stopped
    logstash: # name is also unique and will match with docker images inside Dockerfile/yml files too; could include special chars etc but not recommended because they're likely to conflict in the compose file. same process as before, replace image by your preferred ELK stack version or a tag
      image: logstash:latest # similar reason above like elk_stack only changes here if different use case is needed for Logstash/ELK Stack — else it should remain without change unless you wish to override the default Elasticsearch Image. Same process as before, providing correct details in this area too
      network_mode: "<your custom service's internal port>" # same explanation with elk services above regarding ports for these two or other need not be mentioned if there is no external connectivity required otherwise specific requirements would explain it more clearly below. – should only use when you have any kind of containerized data exchange over network,
      restart_policy: {}    # as described in the previous paragraph about restarts and retries for these two services also needs to be mentioned here too if they require anything else than default one defined by Docker itself otherwise commented out. – not recommended unless required because it might have security implications or due specific use-case
      depends_on: # define other dependent/prerequisites (other containers) for Logstash and ELK stack to start in order before starting Elasticsearch & Kibana as shown below, same explanation above. only uncomment if necessary otherwise no change should be made unless you have specific use-case requirements here
        - elk_stack  # replace with other service's name or ID which depends on your application needs and it must match id in docker run command of the dependent services as defined below for Logstash & ELK stack. For example: ["elk_start", "logstash"] , not necessary if there is no dependencies
      environment: # define all env vars related to these service here, usually used during development but can also be done outside compose file or in docker run command as well which are then accessible within the container itself via -e flag. – optional and depends on use-case whether you want this part is needed otherwise no need for commenting out such section
        # add your environment vars here, e.g: ELK_PASSWORD=xxxxxx , not recommended unless there's specific reason mentioned above because it could be a security risk – should only uncomment if necessary and provide required info like -e VARIABLENAME or other variable pairs which are then accessible inside the service using $VARIABLES
      restart: "no" # Specify how services restarts when running out of resources (CPU/memory usage, etc.), in general we do not recommend this because you can have multiple instances of same image and these images could cause different behavior depending on your requirements. In other words the use case is to provide explicit control over restarting processes as defined by Docker itself but recommended for development purpose or when required due security concerns (mandatory uncomment if necessary)
      volumes: # Define data persistence in container, not mandatory nor provided here because it depends whether you want this part. Might be used while using other services too like Logstash which could also have a persistent volume defined with specific mount point and options for both read/write access to the host (mandatory uncomment if necessary)
        # add your volumes details, e.g: -v /data:/var/lib/elasticsearch : not recommended unless you want additional persistence in case of fail or other needs as explained above due security concerns – should only be used when required and provide appropriate mount points for host machine directory which can hold data
      networks: # define the network connection, this depends whether your services are within a single custom docker networking (same name specified) so it is not mentioned in detail here but recommended to use as per requirement. The same comment about ports should be provided regarding ELK-stack or Logstash and Kibana setup if you have specific external connectivity requirements
        - <your_custom_network> # replace with your network's name, this will ensure communication within the docker networks only when we provide a custom setting otherwise default (serviceName) is used. If not specified then service running in same host machine could access other services via their respective internal ports or using normal URL’s if needed
      labels: {} # define extra metadata for your application/container, this depends whether it're necessary and might be added to enable auto discovery of docker containers with certain tags – should not provide unless required. The same comment about ELK-stack setup could also apply here because the network ports or URLs can use these labels as filter
        # add metadata like: - "com.example.app_name=myApp", if needed else leave it empty for now (mandatory uncomment and specify, e.g., "- com.example.env=dev") – this part is not recommended to enable auto discovery due security concerns or when required by your specific requirements
